channel.title: m-files_corporation — recent CVEs
channel.description: Most recent CVEs assigned by m-files_corporation (m-files_corporation).

item.title: CVE-2026-0983 · 7.1 HIGH — Denial of service vulnerability in M-Files Server
item.description: Denial-of-service condition in M-Files Server versions before 26.5.16015.0, before 26.2 LTS, and before 25.8 LTS SR3 allows an authenticated user to cause the MFserver process to crash

item.title: CVE-2026-0932 · 6.9 MEDIUM 🌩️
item.description: Blind server-side request forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in legacy connection methods of document co-authoring features in M-Files Server before 26.3 allow an unauthenticated attacker to cause the server to send HTTP GET requests to arbitrary URLs.

item.title: CVE-2026-0663 · 6.9 MEDIUM — Denial of Service condition in M-Files Server
item.description: Denial-of-service vulnerability in M-Files Server versions before 26.1.15632.3 allows an authenticated attacker with vault administrator privileges to crash the M-Files Server process by calling a vulnerable API endpoint.

item.title: CVE-2025-13008 · 8.6 HIGH — Session Token Disclosure in M-Files Web
item.description: An information disclosure vulnerability in M-Files Server before versions 25.12.15491.7, 25.8 LTS SR3, 25.2 LTS SR3 and 24.8 LTS SR5 allows an authenticated attacker using M-Files Web to capture session tokens of other active users.

item.title: CVE-2025-14267 · 5.6 MEDIUM — Unintended temporary cached data included in a structure only copy intended to be empty of data
item.description: Incomplete removal of sensitive information before transfer vulnerability in M-Files Corporation M-Files Server allows data leak exposure affecting versions before 25.12.15491.7

item.title: CVE-2025-14318 · 5.3 MEDIUM — Improper access validation in M-Files Server
item.description: Improper access checks in M-Files Server before 25.12.15491.7 allows users to download files through M-Files Web using Web Companion despite Print and Download Prevention module being enabled.

item.title: CVE-2025-11681 · 7.1 HIGH — Denial of Service condition in M-Files Server
item.description: Denial-of-service condition in M-Files Server versions before 25.11.15392.1, before 25.2 LTS SR2 and before 25.8 LTS SR2 allows an authenticated user to cause the MFserver process to crash.

item.title: CVE-2025-9826 · 7 HIGH
item.description: Stored cross-site scripting vulnerability in M-Files Hubshare before version 25.8 allows authenticated attackers to cause script execution for other users.

item.title: CVE-2025-2091 · 4.8 MEDIUM — Open redirection in M-Files Mobile
item.description: An open redirection vulnerability in M-Files mobile applications for Android and iOS prior to version 25.6.0 allows attackers to use maliciously crafted PDF files to trick other users into making requests to untrusted URLs.

item.title: CVE-2025-5964 · 8.4 HIGH — Path traversal in M-Files API
item.description: A path traversal issue in the API endpoint in M-Files Server before version 25.6.14925.0 allows an authenticated user to read files in the server.

item.title: CVE-2025-3087 · 5.1 MEDIUM — Stored XSS Vulnerability in M-Files Web
item.description: Stored XSS in M-Files Web versions from 25.1.14445.5 to 25.2.14524.4 allows an authenticated user to run scripts

item.title: CVE-2025-3086 · 6.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — User in anonymous role could create and delete views
item.description: Improper isolation of users in M-Files Server version before 25.3.14549 allows anonymous user to affect other anonymous users views and possibly cause a denial of service

item.title: CVE-2025-2159 · 5.1 MEDIUM — Stored XSS in M-Files Admin user interface
item.description: Stored XSS in Desktop UI in M-Files Server Admin tool before version 25.3.14681.7 on Windows allows authenticated local user to run scripts via UI

item.title: CVE-2025-0635 · 6.3 MEDIUM 🌩️ — Denial of Service condition in M-Files Server
item.description: Denial of service condition in M-Files Server in versions before 25.1.14445.5 allows an unauthenticated user to consume computing resources in certain conditions.

item.title: CVE-2025-0619 · 4.6 MEDIUM — Unsafe stored password recovery
item.description: Unsafe password recovery from configuration in M-Files Server before 25.1 allows a highly privileged user to recover external connector passwords

item.title: CVE-2025-0648 · 5.9 MEDIUM — M-Files Server crash via EOT database driver configuration
item.description: Unexpected server crash in database driver in M-Files Server before 25.1.14445.5 and before 24.8 LTS SR3 allows a highly privileged attacker to cause denial of service via configuration change.

item.title: CVE-2024-10126 · 5.3 MEDIUM — Local file inclusion vulnerability in M-Files Server
item.description: Local File Inclusion vulnerability in M-Files Server in versions before 24.11 (excluding 24.8 SR1, 24.2 SR3 and 23.8 SR7) allows an authenticated user to read server local files of a limited set of filetypes via document preview.

item.title: CVE-2024-10127 · 9.2 CRITICAL 🌩️ — Support for authentication bypass condition in M-Files LDAP authentication
item.description: Authentication bypass condition in LDAP authentication in M-Files server versions before 24.11 supported usage of OpenLDAP configurations that allowed user authentication without a password when the LDAP server itself had the vulnerable configuration…

item.title: CVE-2024-11176 · 5.3 MEDIUM ☁️ — Incorrect evaluation of effective permissions in M-Files Aino
item.description: Improper access control vulnerability in M-Files Aino in versions before 24.10 allowed an authenticated user to access object information via incorrect evaluation of effective permissions.

item.title: CVE-2024-9333 · 5.3 MEDIUM — Permission bypass in M-Files Connector for Copilot
item.description: Permissions bypass in M-Files Connector for Copilot before version 24.9.3 allows authenticated user to access limited amount of documents via incorrect access control list calculation

item.title: CVE-2024-9174 · 6.9 MEDIUM — Stored HTML Injection in Hubshare social module
item.description: Stored HTML Injection in Social Module in M-Files Hubshare before version 5.0.8.6 allows authenticated user to spoof UI

item.title: CVE-2024-6789 · 8.4 HIGH — Path traversal in M-Files API
item.description: A path traversal issue in API endpoint in M-Files Server before version 24.8.13981.0 and LTS 24.2.13421.15 SR2 and LTS 23.8.12892.0 SR6 allows authenticated user to read files

item.title: CVE-2024-6124 · 8.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Reflected XSS in Hubshare via Open Redirect
item.description: Reflected XSS in M-Files Hubshare before version 5.0.6.0 allows an attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript code in the context of the victim's browser session

item.title: CVE-2024-6881 · 8.5 HIGH — Stored XSS Vulnerability
item.description: Stored XSS in M-Files Hubshare versions before 5.0.6.0 allows an authenticated attacker to execute arbitrary JavaScript in user's browser session

item.title: CVE-2024-5142 · 7 HIGH — XSS in Hubshare's social module
item.description: Stored Cross-Site Scripting vulnerability in Social Module in M-Files Hubshare before version 5.0.6.0 allows authenticated attacker to run scripts in other users browser

item.title: CVE-2024-4056 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Denial of service condition in M-Files Server
item.description: Denial of service condition in M-Files Server in versions before 24.4.13592.4 and after 23.11 (excluding 24.2 LTS) allows unauthenticated user to consume computing resources.

item.title: CVE-2023-4479 · 7.3 HIGH — Stored XSS Vulnerability in M-Files Web
item.description: Stored XSS Vulnerability in M-Files Web versions before 23.8 allows attacker to execute script on users browser via stored HTML document within limited time period.

item.title: CVE-2024-0563 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Denial of service condition in M-Files Server
item.description: Denial of service condition in M-Files Server in versions before 24.2 (excluding 23.2 SR7 and 23.8 SR5) allows anonymous user to cause denial of service against other anonymous users.

item.title: CVE-2023-6910 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Uncontrolled Resource Consumption in M-Files Server
item.description: A vulnerable API method in M-Files Server before 23.12.13195.0 allows for uncontrolled resource consumption. Authenticated attacker can exhaust server storage space to a point where the server can no longer serve requests.

item.title: CVE-2023-6912 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Brute force vulnerability in M-Files user authentication
item.description: Lack of protection against brute force attacks in M-Files Server before 23.12.13205.0 allows an attacker unlimited authentication attempts, potentially compromising targeted M-Files user accounts by guessing passwords.

item.title: CVE-2023-6239 · 5.4 MEDIUM — Incorrect calculation of effective permissions
item.description: Under rare conditions, the effective permissions of an object might be incorrectly calculated if the object has a specific configuration of metadata-driven permissions in M-Files Server versions 23.9, 23.10, and 23.11 before 23.11.13168.7, potentiall…

item.title: CVE-2023-6189 · 4.3 MEDIUM — Improper Permission Handling in M-Files Server
item.description: Missing access permissions checks in the M-Files server before 23.11.13156.0 allow attackers to perform data write and export jobs using the M-Files API methods.

item.title: CVE-2023-6117 · 5.7 MEDIUM — M-Files REST API allows Denial of Service
item.description: A possibility of unwanted server memory consumption was detected through the obsolete functionalities in the Rest API methods of the M-Files server before 23.11.13156.0 which allows attackers to execute DoS attacks.

item.title: CVE-2023-5524 · 8.2 HIGH — M-Files Web Companion allows Remote Code Execution for some filetypes
item.description: Insufficient blacklisting in M-Files Web Companion before release version 23.10 and LTS Service Release Versions before 23.8 LTS SR1 allows Remote Code Execution via specific file types

item.title: CVE-2023-5523 · 8.6 HIGH — M-Files Web Companion allows Remote Code Execution
item.description: Execution of downloaded content flaw in M-Files Web Companion before release version 23.10 and LTS Service Release Versions before 23.8 LTS SR1 allows Remote Code Execution

item.title: CVE-2023-2325 · 7.3 HIGH — Stored XSS Vulnerability in M-Files Classic Web
item.description: Stored XSS Vulnerability in M-Files Classic Web versions before 23.10 and LTS Service Release Versions before 23.2 LTS SR4 and 23.8 LTS SR1allows attacker to execute script on users browser via stored HTML document.

item.title: CVE-2023-3406 · 7.7 HIGH — Path traversal issue in M-Files Classic Web
item.description: Path Traversal issue in M-Files Classic Web versions below 23.6.12695.3 and LTS Service Release Versions before 23.2 LTS SR3 allows authenticated user to read some restricted files on the web server

item.title: CVE-2023-3425 · 6.5 MEDIUM 🌩️ — CVE-2023-3425: Out-of-Bounds memory read
item.description: Out-of-bounds read issue in M-Files Server versions below 23.8.12892.6 and LTS Service Release Versions before 23.2 LTS SR3 allows unauthenticated user to read restricted amount of bytes from memory.

item.title: CVE-2023-3405 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Denial of service condition in M-Files Server
item.description: Unchecked parameter value in M-Files Server in versions before 23.6.12695.3 (excluding 23.2 SR2 and newer) allows anonymous user to cause denial of service

item.title: CVE-2023-2480 · 7.5 HIGH — Elevation of Privilege in M-Files Desktop Client
item.description: Missing access permissions checks in M-Files Client before 23.5.12598.0 (excluding 23.2 SR2 and newer) allows elevation of privilege via UI extension applications

item.title: CVE-2023-2112 · 3.6 LOW — Desktop component allows lateral movement between sessions
item.description: Desktop component service allows lateral movement between sessions in M-Files before 23.4.12455.0.

item.title: CVE-2023-0384 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Uncontrolled Resource Consuption in M-Files Server
item.description: User-controlled operations could have allowed Denial of Service in M-Files Server before 23.4.12528.1 due to uncontrolled memory consumption for a scheduled job.

item.title: CVE-2023-0383 · 7.5 HIGH 🌩️ — Uncontrolled Resource Consuption in M-Files Server
item.description: User-controlled operations could have allowed Denial of Service in M-Files Server before 23.4.12528.1 due to uncontrolled memory consumption.

item.title: CVE-2023-0382 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Uncontrolled Resource Consumption in M-Files Server
item.description: User-controlled operations could have allowed Denial of Service in M-Files Server before 23.4.12528.1 due to uncontrolled memory consumption.

item.title: CVE-2023-0213 · 8.8 HIGH — Local Elevation of Privilege in M-Files
item.description: Elevation of privilege issue in M-Files Installer versions before 22.6 on Windows allows user to gain SYSTEM privileges via DLL hijacking.

item.title: CVE-2022-4862 · 5 MEDIUM — XSS vulnerability in M-Files Web
item.description: Rendering of HTML provided by another authenticated user is possible in browser on M-Files Web before 22.12.12140.3. This allows the content to steal user sensitive information. This issue affects M-Files New Web: before 22.12.12140.3.

item.title: CVE-2022-3284 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Insecure way of passing a download key
item.description: Download key for a file in a vault was passed in an insecure way that could easily be logged in M-Files New Web in M-Files before 22.11.12011.0. This issue affects M-Files New Web: before 22.11.12011.0.

item.title: CVE-2022-4861 · 4.8 MEDIUM — Incorrect Implementation of Authentication Algorithm
item.description: Incorrect implementation in authentication protocol in M-Files Client before 22.5.11356.0 allows high privileged user to get other users tokens to another resource.

item.title: CVE-2022-4858 · 4.4 MEDIUM — Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log File
item.description: Insertion of Sensitive Information into Log Files in M-Files Server before 22.10.11846.0 could allow to obtain sensitive tokens from logs, if specific configurations were set.

item.title: CVE-2022-4264 · 6.5 MEDIUM — Incorrect privilege assignment in M-Files Web Server
item.description: Incorrect Privilege Assignment in M-Files Web (Classic) in M-Files before 22.8.11691.0 allows low privilege user to change some configuration.
